name: Dedup production command validation
summary: Add a production-shaped integration check for the real sorted pairsam dedup command contract.
goal: Validate the exact `pairs-rs dedup` command class used by the real pipeline, including output files, compressed duplicate/unmapped streams, stats keys, DD marking, pairsam SAM duplicate flags/Yt tags, and readID routing against Python pairtools.
explicit_non_goals: Do not implement new dedup features.
Do not optimize dedup or claim performance.
Do not implement select, split, stats, restrict, phase, sample, scaling, filterbycov, parse2, or header behavior.
Do not claim full pairtools dedup parity from this validation.
Do not call Python pairtools at Rust runtime.
candidate_commands: pairs-rs dedup --mark-dups --output-stats merged.dedup.s01.RS.stats.txt --output-dups merged.dups.pairsam.s01.RS.gz --output-unmapped merged.unmapped.pairsam.s01.RS.gz -o nodups.parse_RS_s01.sorted.pairsam H1_ALL_parse_RS_1.sorted_2.pairsam
acceptance_criteria: The production-shaped `pairs-rs dedup` command exits 0 on a pipeline-style sorted pairsam fixture.
The nodups file exists and contains non-header rows.
The duplicate and unmapped `.gz` outputs validate with BGZF or gzip tooling.
The stats file contains total, total_mapped, total_unmapped, total_dups, total_nodups, and fraction_dups.
Duplicate output rows have pair_type DD under `--mark-dups`.
Pairsam duplicate SAM records have duplicate flag 0x400 and Yt:Z:DD where SAM columns exist.
ReadID routing for nodups, dups, and unmapped matches Python pairtools dedup on the same fixture.
known_risks: This validates the production command shape on a small pipeline-style fixture, not dedup optimization or full pairtools parity.
